title: tk roadmap command: render epic chains with status and gate badges
description:
Spec: docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md §1 (tk roadmap bullet). Consumes query.Roadmap from internal/query/roadmap.go (foundation tick, merged before this runs — read its exported types first; statuses: done|active|ready|queued|gated).

New file: cmd/tk/cmd/roadmap.go (+ roadmap_test.go). Follow graph.go conventions (styles via internal/styles, --json flag, cobra registration in init()).

Human output: waves of epics in dependency order, one line per epic:
  <status glyph> <id>  <title>   [<closed>/<total> ticks]  <gate badge if gated: awaiting:<type>>
with a legend line and a final summary (N epics: x done, y active, ...). Use the same glyph/color conventions tk list uses for statuses; ready epics get a "needs planning" annotation (consistent with tk graph wording).

JSON output (--json): marshal the query.Roadmap struct directly — additive, no reshaping.

Scope flag: tk roadmap with no args shows all epics; tk roadmap <epic-id> shows only the chain (transitive blockers + dependents) containing that epic.

Test: unit tests for the chain-scoping filter and output assembly where testable; end-to-end fixture in /tmp (chain of 3 epics in different states) — document commands in your report.

Run: go test ./... && go build ./...
acceptance_criteria: go test ./... passes; tk roadmap renders waves with statuses+badges on a fixture and --json emits the query.Roadmap shape
